workItem.title: Canonicalize ANSI audit events and recover rejected outbox batches

## Context

Live recovery proved that Jason emitted uppercase hexadecimal control escapes such as \\u001B while RFC 8785/game-api requires lowercase \\u001b. The first ANSI-bearing command result received audit_event_hash_invalid and blocked all later outbox batches. A validated candidate exists at commit 31b224b on fork branch fix/audit-outbox-rebase and is installed on X1 for recovery proof.

## Expected outcome

Symphony generates byte-identical RFC 8785 hashes for all command output and automatically rebases/recomputes unconfirmed batches after hash or chain rejection, without changing eventId or operationId.

## Scope

- Land and independently review candidate commit 31b224b
- Verify ANSI control escape fixture against game-api RFC8785
- Handle 422 audit hash/canonicalization rejection as recoverable local rehash
- Preserve atomic rebase and receipt semantics
- Prove the live bos-front#2 outbox drains and its remote chain verifies
- Publish a policy-compliant PR

## Out of scope

- Rewrite confirmed GitHub audit history
- Discard failed command output

## Acceptance criteria

- ANSI-bearing event canonical bytes match Python rfc8785
- 422 audit_event_hash_invalid triggers bounded rehash/rebase
- All pending bos-front#2 events are confirmed without identity loss
- Remote run chain verifies from sequence 1 through the recovered tail
- make all passes
- Independent reviews and exact-head evidence pass
